title: 10 best herbal teas for the common cold

remedies.remedy_desc: Elderberry tea helps with the common cold because it contains antioxidants and vitamins that boost the immune system, such as Vitamin C, A, and bioflavonoids. These compounds can help reduce inflammation, ease swelling in mucous membranes, and alleviate nasal congestion. Furthermore, elderberry contains antiviral properties, which may prevent the cold virus from replicating and shorten the duration of symptoms. Additionally, the soothing warmth of the tea can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate coughing, providing overall relief of cold symptoms. However, it is important to consume elderberry tea that has been properly prepared, as raw or unripe elderberries can be toxic.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Elderflowers are rich in antioxidants and can help alleviate symptoms of the common cold by reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system.
Berries: Elderberries contain high levels of vitamin C, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which have antiviral properties and can help shorten the duration of a cold by fighting off viruses.
Leaves: Elder leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat fever, and they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and relieve congestion.
Bark: The inner bark of the elderberry tree has been used to make teas that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce fever associated with the common cold.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These are powerful antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and boost the immune system, protecting the body from catching a cold.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids found in elderberries have antiviral properties, preventing the replication of the cold virus in the body.
Vitamin C: Elderberries are rich in vitamin C, which strengthens the immune system and helps the body fight off the common cold.
Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids in elderberries have antimicrobial properties, helping the body to eliminate any potential pathogens causing a cold.
Carotenoids: Carotenoids found in elderberries are antioxidants that help protect the body from oxidative stress due to infections such as the common cold.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 1 cup of water and 1-2 tablespoons of dried elderberries; bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, let simmer for 15-20 minutes to extract the berry's medicinal properties.
Add honey and lemon juice to taste, stir well until fully dissolved.
Drink warm, up to 3 times a day for 3-5 days to alleviate cold symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with the common cold because it contains menthol, a natural decongestant that helps to break up phlegm and thin mucus, making it easier to expel. Additionally, the warmth of the tea can help to soothe a sore throat and alleviate congestion. Peppermint tea also has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the throat and nasal passages. Furthermore, peppermint tea has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial effects, which may help the body to fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause the common cold. Lastly, drinking peppermint tea can help to keep the body hydrated, which is important for overall health and can help to support the immune system in fighting off illness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and rosmarinic acid, peppermint leaves are commonly used to make medicinal tea for the common cold due to their ability to alleviate symptoms such as congestion and cough.

Flowers: Packed with flavonoids and volatile oils, peppermint flowers contribute to the soothing effects of medicinal tea, providing relief for sore throats and inflammation caused by the common cold.
Seeds: High in essential oils, peppermint seeds can be used to enhance the therapeutic properties of medicinal tea, supporting digestion and easing symptoms of nausea associated with the common cold.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as a decongestant and helps to break up phlegm and mucus, making it easier to expel from the respiratory tract.
Mentone: Has antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help the body fight off the cold virus.
Limonene: Has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and throat caused by a cold.
Rosmarinic acid: Has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help boost the immune system and reduce symptoms of a cold.
flavonoids: Has antioxidant and antiviral properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by the cold virus and reduce symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a tea infuser or teapot, place one peppermint tea bag for every 8 ounces of water.
Pour boiling water over the tea bag(s) and let steep for 5-7 minutes for optimal flavor and health benefits.
Remove the tea bag(s) and optionally add honey or lemon to taste.
Pour the peppermint tea into a cup, ensuring it is still hot.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe a sore throat and ease congestion associated with the common cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with the common cold because it contains antiviral and antibacterial properties that aid in fighting off the virus and reducing inflammation. The warm temperature of the tea can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate congestion by expanding the airways in the nose. Ginger tea is also known to boost the immune system due to its high antioxidant content, which can help the body combat the cold virus more efficiently. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have a pain-relieving effect, which can help reduce the discomfort and body aches often associated with the common cold. Lastly, ginger tea can help rehydrate and refresh the body, which is essential for recovery and overall health.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem structure of ginger, called rhizomes, is used in medicinal tea for the common cold due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are used in tea to alleviate symptoms of the common cold because they contain compounds with antimicrobial and analgesic effects.
Flowers: Ginger flowers can be used in medicinal tea for the common cold as they have been found to have antiviral and expectorant properties.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are sometimes included in tea for the common cold as they may help to boost the immune system and provide relief from congestion.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, the most abundant compounds in ginger, have potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate symptoms of the common cold by fighting inflammation, reducing pain, and inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria.

Shogaols: Shogaols, derived from gingerols when ginger is dried or cooked, possess strong antiviral and antimicrobial activities which can help provide relief from the common cold by inhibiting viral replication and reducing the severity of symptoms.

Paradols: Paradols, formed from gingerols and shogaols upon exposure to air or heat, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help manage cold symptoms like cough, sore throat, and congestion by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.

Zingerone: Zingerone, another compound formed when ginger is cooked or dried, functions as a natural analgesic, reducing pain and fever associated with the common cold, and may also offer antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, helping to alleviate symptoms and protect against infection.

Vitamin C: While not a unique constituent of ginger, this essential nutrient is abundant in fresh ginger and contributes to the overall cold-fighting properties of ginger by supporting the immune system, reducing inflammation, and providing antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by the common cold.
remedy_recipe: Peel and grate 1 inch of fresh ginger, then steep in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and the juice of half a lemon to the hot ginger water.
Optional: add a cinnamon stick or a few whole cloves for added spice and cold-fighting benefits.
Strain the tea, then sip and enjoy while warm, up to three times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with the common cold because it has antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The herbal tea is known to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off the cold virus. Additionally, chamomile contains a flavonoid called apigenin, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. Furthermore, drinking hot liquids like chamomile tea can help relieve congestion and alleviate coughing fits, providing comfort and promoting restorative sleep, both of which are crucial for recovery. Lastly, chamomile's soothing properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can be exacerbated during cold symptoms, ultimately aiding in an overall improvement in one's condition.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The chamomile flowers are commonly used due to their rich flavonoids and terpenoids content, which are known to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, beneficial for soothing a sore throat and alleviating cold symptoms.
Leaves: Chamomile leaves contain high levels of phenolic compounds, providing antispasmodic and relaxant effects, helpful in easing congestion and promoting a good night's sleep during a cold.

Seeds: Chamomile seeds are a great source of essential oils, including Chamazulene and Bisabolol, which have calming effects and may help reduce fever and inflammation associated with the common cold.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help fight off the viruses and bacteria that cause the common cold, and alleviate symptoms like inflammation and redness.
Chamazulene: This compound has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation, ease coughing and soothe a sore throat.
Bisabolol: This terpene has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of the common cold such as pain, inflammation, and congestion.
Matricin: This compound is a precursor to chamazulene and has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms like coughing and chest tightness.
Spiroether: This compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the body from free radicals, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system during a cold.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water to 212°F (100°C) for 1-2 minutes to release oxygen and improve taste.
Pour the hot water over 1-2 teaspoons (1-2 g) of dried chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Steep the chamomile flowers in the hot water for 5-10 minutes to extract the desired flavors and medicinal compounds.
Remove the chamomile flowers from the water to prevent over-steeping and bitterness.
Sweeten with honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink warm for soothing relief of cold symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea tea helps with the common cold because it is known to boost the immune system, thereby helping the body fight off the virus that causes the cold. The tea is made from the leaves and flowers of the echinacea plant, which contain phenols, a type of antioxidant that supports the immune system and reduces inflammation. Furthermore, echinacea has been found to reduce the duration of a cold by a day or two, and may also alleviate symptoms like sore throat, cough, and fever. However, it is important to note that echinacea's efficacy is still a subject of ongoing research, and it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe cases of the common cold. Lastly, echinacea tea is generally considered safe for most people, but it's advisable to consult with a healthcare provider before using it, particularly for those with certain health conditions or who are taking prescription medications.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of echinacea contain beneficial compounds that can help boost the immune system and alleviate symptoms of the common cold.
Leaves: The leaves of echinacea have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat and reduce fever.
Flowers: The flowers of echinacea are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and boost the immune system, helping to combat the common cold.
Buds: The buds of echinacea are a concentrated source of the plant's beneficial compounds, making them an ideal addition to medicinal tea for the common cold.
remedy_constituents: Alkamides: These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating properties, helping to reduce inflammation and boost the immune response during a common cold.
Cichoric Acid: This polyphenol acts as an antioxidant, protecting cells from damage and supporting the immune system in fighting off the common cold.
Essential Oils: The volatile oils in echinacea, such as eugenol and caryophyllene, have antimicrobial properties, helping to eliminate the virus causing the common cold.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells and helping the body to fight off the common cold.
Vitamin C: Echinacea contains small amounts of vitamin C, which is an essential nutrient for a healthy immune system and can help reduce the severity and duration of a common cold.
remedy_recipe: In a saucepan, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea flowers and/or leaves.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a teapot or cup, discarding the spent echinacea.
Enjoy the warm tea up to three times a day while experiencing cold symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with the common cold because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that soothe throat irritation and help loosen mucus. Furthermore, it helps to boost the immune system by increasing the levels of interferons, proteins that fight viral infections. Additionally, licorice tea can help reduce the duration and severity of cold symptoms due to its antiviral and antibacterial effects. Moreover, it can alleviate symptoms such as cough, congestion, and sore throat, making it an effective natural remedy for the common cold. Lastly, licorice tea is a popular herbal tea that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its soothing and calming effects can provide comfort and relief during a common cold.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which helps soothe sore throats and reduce irritation.
Rhizomes: Licorice rhizomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of the common cold.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and expectorant properties, helpful in treating cold symptoms.
Flowers: Licorice flowers can help stimulate the immune system and have been used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory ailments.
Seeds: Licorice seeds have been used in traditional medicine to treat coughs and congestion, making them useful in medicinal tea for the common cold.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin is the main active component of licorice, and has been shown to have anti-viral and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of the common cold.

Flavonoids: These include liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin, and have been shown to have anti-oxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-viral properties, which can help boost the immune system and fight off the common cold.
Coumarins: Such as glabridin, have been shown to have anti-viral, anti-inflammatory, and anti-allergic properties, which can help reduce symptoms of the common cold.
Phenolic acids: Like chlorogenic acid, have been shown to have anti-viral, anti-inflammatory, and anti-oxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of the common cold.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of crushed licorice root with 8 ounces of water.
Heat the water and licorice root mixture until it reaches a boil.
Strain the liquid into a cup, discarding the spent licorice root.
Sip the tea while it is still warm, adding honey or lemon to taste if desired.

remedies.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with the common cold because it has anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties that work together to provide relief. The tea's natural compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, can reduce inflammation in the respiratory system, helping to alleviate symptoms like congestion, sore throat, and cough. Additionally, yarrow tea can stimulate perspiration, which helps to cool the body and eliminate toxins, further aiding in the recovery process. Furthermore, its antimicrobial effects may directly target the virus causing the cold, potentially shortening the duration of the illness. Lastly, yarrow tea's soothing and calming properties can promote better sleep, an essential factor in boosting the immune system and facilitating a quicker recovery from a common cold.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the yarrow plant are rich in flavonoids, which are known to help reduce inflammation and ease symptoms of the common cold.
Leaves: The leaves of the yarrow plant contain volatile oils and lactones, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, helpful in treating cold symptoms.
Stems: The stems of the yarrow plant contain tannins, which have astringent properties that can help relieve a runny nose and sore throat.

Seeds: The seeds of the yarrow plant are a good source of essential fatty acids and lipids, which can help boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the body, both beneficial in treating a common cold.
remedy_constituents: Essential oil: The essential oil in yarrow has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce cold symptoms and fight off the infection.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in yarrow have antioxidant and antiviral properties, helping to boost the immune system and combat cold symptoms.
Alkaloids: Certain alkaloids in yarrow, like azulene, have been found to have antipyretic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce fever and inflammation associated with the common cold.

Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds found in yarrow have been shown to have immune-modulating and antitussive effects, helping to alleviate coughs and strengthen the immune response during a cold.
Tannins: Tannins in yarrow have astringent properties, helping to soothe a sore throat and relieve congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest or acquire 2-3 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Crush the dried yarrow gently to release its oils, for 1-2 minutes.
Place the crushed yarrow in a tea infuser or loose in a tea pot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the yarrow and steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser or strain the tea, then drink it up to 3 times a day for cold relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with the common cold because it has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe a sore throat, reduce inflammation, and alleviate coughing. The rosmarinic acid found in sage tea also has antioxidant effects, which can help boost the immune system and speed up recovery. Moreover, drinking warm sage tea can help clear nasal congestion by promoting expectoration and reducing mucus production. Additionally, sage tea's calming properties may help reduce stress and promote better sleep, both of which are essential for a quicker recovery from a cold. However, it is important to note that while sage tea can provide relief for cold symptoms, it is not a substitute for medical treatment, especially for severe or persistent symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, leaves are often used in medicinal tea to help alleviate symptoms of the common cold due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and essential oils, sage flowers can help soothe a sore throat and relieve congestion when used in a medicinal tea.
Stems: High in vitamins and minerals, sage stems can help boost the immune system and provide relief from cold symptoms when consumed as a medicinal tea.
Roots: Known for their antimicrobial properties, sage roots can help fight off the virus causing a cold and reduce its duration when used in a medicinal tea.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and damage caused by the common cold.
Camphor: Camphor has expectorant properties, which can help loosen and remove mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating congestion associated with the common cold.
1,8-Cineole: Also known as eucalyptol, this compound has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve congestion and reduce inflammation caused by the common cold.
Bornyl acetate: This compound has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with the common cold.
α-Pinene: This compound has expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate congestion and reduce inflammation caused by the common cold.
remedy_recipe: Heat 1 cup of water to boiling in a tea kettle or pot, which takes about 5 minutes.
Crush 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves or use 2 teaspoons of fresh sage leaves and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the crushed sage leaves and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the sage leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink the soothing sage tea to help alleviate cold symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with the common cold because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery when mixed with water and can soothe irritated throats. This soothing effect can reduce coughing and make it easier to swallow. Additionally, marshmallow tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms such as a sore throat and congestion. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to clear nasal passages and provide relief. Furthermore, marshmallow tea is caffeine-free, making it a good choice for those who want to rest and recover. However, it is important to note that while marshmallow tea can help to alleviate symptoms, it is not a cure for the common cold and medical attention should be sought if symptoms persist for an extended period of time.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, marshmallow flowers are used to make medicinal tea to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation caused by the common cold.
Leaves: High in mucilage, marshmallow leaves are utilized in teas to relieve congestion and coughs, providing a protective coating to irritated mucous membranes.
Roots: Packed with starch, marshmallow roots can be used in teas to alleviate irritation in the digestive tract, often a secondary symptom of the common cold.
remedy_constituents: Mucilage: The mucilage in marshmallow forms a slick gel when combined with water, which can soothe and protect inflamed mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, providing relief from irritating common cold symptoms.

Flavonoids: Marshmallow flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory system, potentially alleviating common cold symptoms.

Ascorbic acid: Ascorbic acid, found in marshmallow, is an essential nutrient for a healthy immune system. It can boost the body's ability to fight off common cold viruses and reduce the severity of cold symptoms.
Pectin: The pectin content in marshmallow can help soothe sore throats, coughs, and other irritations in the respiratory tract that are associated with the common cold.
Tannins: Marshmallow tannins have astringent properties that can help dry up a runny nose and reduce mucus production, offering relief from common cold symptoms.

Polysaccharides: Marshmallow polysaccharides can help boost the immune system, increase mucosal defenses, and inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses, all of which can help the body fight off the common cold.
remedy_recipe: Dissolve 1 tablespoon of marshmallow root in 1 cup of boiling water, letting it steep for 10 minutes to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the tea to remove the marshmallow root pieces, leaving only the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the marshmallow tea, stirring until fully dissolved to sweeten and soothe a sore throat.
Warm up the tea if it has cooled down, but do not boil it again as it can destroy the healing properties.
Sip the marshmallow tea slowly, relaxing and allowing the herbal remedy to alleviate symptoms of the common cold.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with the common cold because it has antibacterial and antioxidant properties that can reduce phlegm and alleviate coughs. The herb's compound, thymol, has been shown to have antimicrobial effects, which may help in fighting off the cold-causing viruses. Moreover, thyme tea is an excellent natural anti-inflammatory agent, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation in the airways caused by cold. Additionally, drinking warm thyme tea can help relieve congestion, clear the nasal passages, and promote relaxation, enabling better sleep, which is crucial for a quicker recovery. Lastly, thyme tea is a rich source of vitamin C and other essential minerals, which can help boost the immune system and improve the body's ability to fight off infections.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in aromatic oils, including thymol, which can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate coughs associated with the common cold.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids that have antioxidant properties, which can help support the immune system during cold symptoms.
Stems: Although less potent than the leaves, thyme stems can still contribute to the tea's medicinal properties, providing a milder but helpful impact on cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antiseptic and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce the growth of harmful bacteria and viruses in the body, providing relief from symptoms of the common cold.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has antispasmodic properties that can help relieve cough and muscle spasms, providing relief from symptoms of the common cold.
Geraniol: Geraniol has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the throat and nasal passages, providing relief from symptoms of the common cold.
Linalool: Linalool has calming and sedative properties that can help promote relaxation and better sleep, which is important for aiding recovery from the common cold.
Terpinen-4-ol: Terpinen-4-ol has antioxidant properties that can help boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress, which can help the body fight off the cold virus.
remedy_recipe: In a small pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil, which takes about 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried thyme leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh thyme to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the thyme leaves.
Optionally, add honey or lemon to taste, then sip and enjoy the soothing effects of your homemade thyme tea.
